purpose: Documentation page for the ide area: Code Explorer (IDE) Architecture Overview. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: The Code Explorer is Synapse's built-in IDE, accessible via a tab in the sidebar alongside the Dashboards tab. It provides a file explorer, Monaco-powered code editor with breakpoint support, multi-workspace tabs, an integrated Node.js debugger with VS Code-style debug panels, syntax diagnostics, and a bridge to link workspaces with dashboards for integrated Claude chat. The file is 386 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
